05 — Survival Analysis

Tests whether the combination of hot/cold immune phenotype and p16/CDKN2A status predicts patient overall survival in TCGA.

Analyses:

  1. Kaplan-Meier: hot vs cold
  2. Kaplan-Meier: 4-group (hot/cold × p16-high/low)
  3. Sensitivity: per-tumor-type log-rank
  4. Cox proportional hazards: adjusted for tumor type via strata
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(broom)

DATA    <- "../data"
FIGURES <- normalizePath("../figures", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(FIGURES, showWarnings = FALSE)

1. Load & Merge Data

immune <- read_parquet(file.path(DATA, "sample_immune_labels.parquet")) |>
  rename(sample_id = `__index_level_0__`)
senes  <- read_parquet(file.path(DATA, "sample_senescence.parquet")) |>
  rename(sample_id = `__index_level_0__`)

# Clinical / survival data (Xena format)
# Common survival columns: OS (1=deceased/0=censored), OS.time (days)
clin <- read.table(
  file.path(DATA, "tcga_clinical_survival.tsv"),
  sep = "\t", header = TRUE, row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
cat("Clinical columns:", paste(colnames(clin), collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Clinical columns: X_PATIENT, cancer.type.abbreviation, age_at_initial_pathologic_diagnosis, gender, race, ajcc_pathologic_tumor_stage, clinical_stage, histological_type, histological_grade, initial_pathologic_dx_year, menopause_status, birth_days_to, vital_status, tumor_status, last_contact_days_to, death_days_to, cause_of_death, new_tumor_event_type, new_tumor_event_site, new_tumor_event_site_other, new_tumor_event_dx_days_to, treatment_outcome_first_course, margin_status, residual_tumor, OS, OS.time, DSS, DSS.time, DFI, DFI.time, PFI, PFI.time, Redaction
OS_COL  <- "OS"
OST_COL <- "OS.time"

master <- immune |>
  inner_join(senes, by = "sample_id") |>
  inner_join(
    clin[, c(OS_COL, OST_COL), drop = FALSE] |>
      tibble::rownames_to_column("sample_id"),
    by = "sample_id"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(.data[[OS_COL]]), !is.na(.data[[OST_COL]])) |>
  mutate(OS.time_years = .data[[OST_COL]] / 365.25)
## Warning in inner_join(immune, senes, by = "sample_id"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 3189 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 104 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
cat("Survival table:", nrow(master), "rows\n")
## Survival table: 9818 rows

2. Classify p16-High/Low Within Tumor Type

# ntile(2) assigns 1=low, 2=high within each tumor type
master <- master |>
  group_by(tumor_type) |>
  mutate(
    p16_status = case_when(
      ntile(CDKN2A_expr, 2) == 2 ~ "p16-high",
      ntile(CDKN2A_expr, 2) == 1 ~ "p16-low",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    group4 = paste(hot_cold, p16_status, sep = " / ")
  )

cat("Group sizes:\n")
## Group sizes:
print(table(master$group4))
## 
## cold / p16-high  cold / p16-low  hot / p16-high   hot / p16-low 
##            2298            2620            2601            2299

3. KM: Hot vs Cold

surv_obj <- Surv(master$OS.time_years, master$OS)

fit_hc <- survfit(surv_obj ~ hot_cold, data = master)

# Log-rank test
lr_hc <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ hot_cold, data = master)
lr_p  <- 1 - pchisq(lr_hc$chisq, df = length(lr_hc$obs) - 1)
cat(sprintf("Log-rank p (hot vs cold): %.2e\n", lr_p))
## Log-rank p (hot vs cold): 5.90e-02
p1 <- ggsurvplot(
  fit_hc,
  data       = master,
  palette    = c("cold" = "#3498db", "hot" = "#e74c3c"),
  pval       = TRUE,
  conf.int   = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  xlab       = "Time (years)",
  title      = sprintf("Overall Survival: Hot vs Cold (log-rank p=%.2e)", lr_p),
  legend.labs = c("cold", "hot")
)

ggsave(file.path(FIGURES, "km_hot_cold.png"),
       plot = print(p1), width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 150)
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's colour values.
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.

p1
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's colour values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.

4. KM: 4-Group (Hot/Cold × p16-High/Low)

sub4 <- master |>
  filter(!is.na(group4), !is.na(OS.time_years), !is.na(OS)) |>
  group_by(group4) |>
  filter(n() >= 30) |>
  ungroup()

surv4 <- Surv(sub4$OS.time_years, sub4$OS)
fit4  <- survfit(surv4 ~ group4, data = sub4)

lr4   <- survdiff(surv4 ~ group4, data = sub4)
lr4_p <- 1 - pchisq(lr4$chisq, df = length(lr4$obs) - 1)
cat(sprintf("Log-rank p (4-group): %.2e\n", lr4_p))
## Log-rank p (4-group): 1.98e-03
group_palette <- c(
  "hot / p16-high"  = "#c0392b",
  "hot / p16-low"   = "#e08080",
  "cold / p16-high" = "#1a5276",
  "cold / p16-low"  = "#85c1e9"
)

p2 <- ggsurvplot(
  fit4,
  data        = sub4,
  palette     = group_palette[levels(factor(sub4$group4))],
  pval        = TRUE,
  conf.int    = FALSE,
  risk.table  = TRUE,
  xlab        = "Time (years)",
  title       = sprintf("OS: Hot/Cold \u00d7 p16-High/Low (log-rank p=%.2e)", lr4_p),
  legend.labs = levels(factor(sub4$group4)),
  fontsize    = 3.5
)
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
ggsave(file.path(FIGURES, "km_4group.png"),
       plot = print(p2), width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 150)
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's colour values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.

p2
## Warning: No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the
## data's fill values.
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Strata"

## No shared levels found between `names(values)` of the manual scale and the

5. Sensitivity: Per-Tumor-Type Log-Rank

per_type_lr <- master |>
  filter(!is.na(hot_cold), !is.na(OS.time_years), !is.na(OS)) |>
  group_by(tumor_type) |>
  filter(n() >= 50) |>
  summarise(
    n_hot    = sum(hot_cold == "hot"),
    n_cold   = sum(hot_cold == "cold"),
    lr_pval  = {
      sd <- survdiff(Surv(OS.time_years, OS) ~ hot_cold,
                     data = cur_data())
      1 - pchisq(sd$chisq, df = 1)
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(padj = p.adjust(lr_pval, method = "BH")) |>
  arrange(lr_pval)
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `summarise()`.
## ℹ In argument: `lr_pval = { ... }`.
## ℹ In group 1: `tumor_type = "adrenocortical cancer"`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! `cur_data()` was deprecated in dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use `pick()` instead.
print(per_type_lr)
## # A tibble: 30 × 5
##    tumor_type                        n_hot n_cold   lr_pval     padj
##    <chr>                             <int>  <int>     <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 kidney papillary cell carcinoma      41    248 0.0000288 0.000865
##  2 sarcoma                             105    154 0.00349   0.0523  
##  3 thymoma                              57     62 0.0110    0.110   
##  4 brain lower grade glioma              9    505 0.0176    0.114   
##  5 uveal melanoma                       10     70 0.0189    0.114   
##  6 esophageal carcinoma                103     81 0.0315    0.157   
##  7 adrenocortical cancer                 5     74 0.0706    0.260   
##  8 skin cutaneous melanoma              52     51 0.0707    0.260   
##  9 kidney clear cell carcinoma         452     93 0.0855    0.260   
## 10 ovarian serous cystadenocarcinoma   192    118 0.0916    0.260   
## # ℹ 20 more rows

6. Cox Proportional Hazards (Adjusted)

cox_data <- master |>
  select(OS.time_years, OS, ifng_score, CDKN2A_expr, tumor_type) |>
  filter(if_all(everything(), Negate(is.na)))

# strata(tumor_type) allows baseline hazard to vary by cancer type
# without fitting one coefficient per type (avoids one-hot explosion)
cox_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(OS.time_years, OS) ~ ifng_score + CDKN2A_expr + strata(tumor_type),
  data = cox_data
)

print(summary(cox_fit))
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(OS.time_years, OS) ~ ifng_score + CDKN2A_expr + 
##     strata(tumor_type), data = cox_data)
## 
##   n= 9818, number of events= 2735 
## 
##                  coef exp(coef)  se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)
## ifng_score  -0.030434  0.970025  0.027657 -1.100    0.271
## CDKN2A_expr -0.009696  0.990351  0.007953 -1.219    0.223
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## ifng_score     0.9700      1.031    0.9188     1.024
## CDKN2A_expr    0.9904      1.010    0.9750     1.006
## 
## Concordance= 0.501  (se = 0.007 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 3.1  on 2 df,   p=0.2
## Wald test            = 3.11  on 2 df,   p=0.2
## Score (logrank) test = 3.11  on 2 df,   p=0.2
# Tidy output
cox_tidy <- tidy(cox_fit, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
print(cox_tidy)
## # A tibble: 2 × 7
##   term        estimate std.error statistic p.value conf.low conf.high
##   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
## 1 ifng_score     0.970   0.0277      -1.10   0.271    0.919      1.02
## 2 CDKN2A_expr    0.990   0.00795     -1.22   0.223    0.975      1.01
# Check proportional hazards assumption
ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_fit)
print(ph_test)
##                chisq df    p
## ifng_score  0.000435  1 0.98
## CDKN2A_expr 0.526308  1 0.47
## GLOBAL      0.545181  2 0.76
cat("\nPH assumption satisfied if all p > 0.05 (non-significant deviation)\n")
## 
## PH assumption satisfied if all p > 0.05 (non-significant deviation)
# Forest plot — ggforest doesn't support strata(), so build manually from tidy output
p3 <- ggplot(cox_tidy, aes(x = estimate, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, y = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#e74c3c") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x     = "Hazard Ratio (95% CI)",
    y     = NULL,
    title = "Cox HR (adjusted for tumor type via strata)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
ggsave(file.path(FIGURES, "cox_forest_plot.png"),
       plot = p3, width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 150)
## `height` was translated to `width`.
p3
## `height` was translated to `width`.

7. Export Tidy Results

write.csv(cox_tidy,    file.path(DATA, "cox_results.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(per_type_lr, file.path(DATA, "logrank_per_type.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("Exported cox_results.csv and logrank_per_type.csv\n")
## Exported cox_results.csv and logrank_per_type.csv
